chapter_title.ar: باب بَدْءِ الْأَذَانِ وَ الْإِقَامَةِ وَ فَضْلِهِمَا وَ ثَوَابِهِمَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 18 – The Azān (Call to the Salāt), and the Iqamah (Call to the establishment of the Salāt), and their merits and their Rewards

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/89)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/89)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْمَرْأَةِ عَلَيْهَا أَذَانٌ وَ إِقَامَةٌ قَالَ لَا .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Al Fazl Bin ShAzān, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Jameel Bin Darraj who said,
‘I asked Abu Abdullahasws about the woman, ‘Is there a recitation of an Azān and an Iqamah upon her?’ Heasws said: ‘No’.
en.sarwar: 18. Muhammad ibn ’Isma‘il has narrated from al-Fadl ibn Shadhan from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Jamil ibn Darraj who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, if a woman is required to say Adhan and ‘Iqamah. He (the Imam) said, ‘No, she is not required.’”
